svg.title: NASA CMAPSS Turbofan Dataset Structure
svg.desc: Diagram showing how the 4 datasets (FD001–FD004) split into train, test, and RUL files, with their columns explained

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What is different between FD001 and FD002 in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What is different about FD002 in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What is different about FD003 in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What is different about FD004 in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What does the train file contain in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What does the RUL file contain in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What is the unit number column in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What is the cycle column in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What do the 3 operational settings mean in the NASA turbofan dataset?')
sendPrompt('What do the 21 sensor columns measure in the NASA turbofan dataset?')

svg.text: Sensors include: fan speed (Nf), core speed (Nc), temperatures (T2, T24, T30, T50),
pressures (P2, P15, P30), fuel flow, bypass ratio, coolant bleed flow, and more.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How do I use the RUL file to build a machine learning model?')
g.text: RUL file — one number per test engine
The true Remaining Useful Life (cycles left) at the last observation in the test file.
Your model predicts this; line N in RUL_FD001.txt matches engine N in test_FD001.txt.
